title: Camera and Image Picker
description: Access the device camera and pick images from the gallery.

slides.title: Why Device Features? 📱
slides.content:
# Making Apps Feel Native

Accessing device hardware (camera, location, sensors) is what makes mobile apps powerful. In React Native, we use community libraries or Expo modules to interact with these features.

For camera and image picking, the most popular library is `expo-image-picker` (for Expo) or `react-native-image-picker` (for bare React Native).

We'll use Expo modules in this course for simplicity.

slides.title: Installing and Permissions
Using ImagePicker

quizData.question: Which function requests camera permissions in expo-image-picker?
quizData.explanation: `requestCameraPermissionsAsync()` is the correct method from expo-image-picker.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Always handle the case where the user cancels (`result.canceled` is true). Also check permissions before launching the camera.
